title: Run a Polars verb pipeline or a SQL query over named inputs
summary: Two modes sharing one `[inputs]` map. Ops mode chains Polars verbs (select / filter / with_columns / sort / group_by / join / rename / drop / distinct / slice / head / tail) against a starting input. Query mode evaluates a SQL string via polars_sql::SQLContext with each input registered as a table. Inputs may carry their own per-input pre-pipeline applied at load. Output is written as CSV or Parquet (SAV write not yet supported).
description: Each [inputs] entry is loaded lazily (CSV / Parquet) or eagerly (SAV via polars-readstat). Declared `format` overrides the extension-based inference. An input may declare its own `ops` array — those verbs are applied to that input at load time, before anything else sees the frame. Ops mode then applies the top-level `ops` pipeline to the `input`-named frame; verbs map 1:1 to LazyFrame methods, and expression-valued fields (`filter.expr`, `with_columns.exprs`, `group_by.agg`) are parsed with polars_sql::sql_expr so you can write SQL-flavoured scalar and aggregate expressions. The `join` verb resolves its `with` against the [inputs] map (honouring that input's pre-pipeline). Query mode skips the verb pipeline entirely and runs a single SQL statement, with each input name as a table. The result is collected into a DataFrame and written to `output`; the format is inferred from the extension (.csv / .parquet / .sav) unless `output_format` is set explicitly.
usage:
[["data-wrangling::run"]]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "w1"
output = "./out.csv"
input = "main"

[inputs.main]
path = "./data.csv"

[[ops]]
verb = "select"
cols = ["id", "x"]

[[ops]]
verb = "filter"
expr = "x > 20"

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Base directory under which `ocinfo/<run_name>_data_wrangling/` is created by the orchestrator.

arguments.name: output
arguments.description: Output file path. Extension (.csv / .parquet / .sav) picks the writer unless `output_format` is set.

arguments.name: output_format
arguments.description: One of csv|sav|parquet. SAV write currently returns `Error::Unsupported`.

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: Named map of InputSpec. At least one entry required. Keys are referenced by top-level `input`, by `join.with`, and (in query mode) by the SQL FROM clause.

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Ops mode: name of the starting input. Omit in query mode.

arguments.name: ops
arguments.description: Ops mode: ordered verb pipeline. Mutually exclusive with `query`.

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: Query mode: SQL evaluated by polars_sql::SQLContext. Mutually exclusive with `input`/`ops`.

arguments.name: output_format_stdout
arguments.description: Override stdout format when invoked directly: human|protocol|quiet.

verbs.name: select
verbs.notes: Keep listed columns in the given order.

verbs.name: rename
verbs.notes: Rename existing columns; values are the new names.

verbs.name: filter
verbs.notes: `expr` is parsed by polars_sql::sql_expr, e.g. `x > 20 AND group = 'a'`.

verbs.name: with_columns
verbs.notes: Each value is a SQL expression aliased to its key.

verbs.name: sort
verbs.notes: `descending` defaults to all-ascending; when supplied it must match `by`'s length.

verbs.name: distinct
verbs.notes: Empty `subset` → dedupe on the full row. Keep-first, stable.

verbs.name: group_by
verbs.notes: SQL aggregates over each group, e.g. `sum(x)`, `count(id)`, `mean(y)`.

verbs.name: join
verbs.notes: `with` must name an entry in [inputs]; that input's pre-pipeline is applied first. Supported how: inner|left|right|full|semi|anti|cross.

notes: Mode exclusivity is enforced at validation time: supply either `input` + `ops` or `query`, not both, and at least one.
Insertion order of the [inputs] map is preserved (IndexMap). Iteration order affects nothing at runtime beyond the order of SQL table registration.
Input format detection priority: declared `format` → path extension. Unknown extensions fail at load. Supported: .csv / .tsv / .txt (CSV), .parquet, .sav / .zsav.
CSV inputs are always read with a header row. CSV outputs write headers. No customisation of delimiter / quoting in 0.1.0.

SAV write currently returns `Error::Unsupported` because derived frames lack the per-column metadata (widths, value labels, missing-value ranges) that spss-io's write_sav requires. Read is fully supported.

Expressions are polars-sql flavour — keyword operators and function names match SQL (`=`, `!=`, `AND`, `OR`, `IS NULL`, `CAST AS`, `COALESCE`, date/string functions). Full precedence list is in the polars-sql docs.
Query mode runs the SQL in a fresh SQLContext each call: every input is registered under its map key, then `ctx.execute(query).collect()`. No multi-statement support.

artifacts.title: Transformed data
artifacts.description: The result of the verb pipeline or SQL query, written as a CSV or Parquet file. Contains only the columns and rows that survived the operations — selects, filters, joins, aggregations, and renames all shape the output.

fields.name: (variable)
fields.description: Output columns depend entirely on the pipeline operations applied. The final column set reflects any selects, renames, joins, or aggregations in the task specification.
